svg.title: VineContainer viewport overlay lifecycle logic
svg.desc: Editor module, selected actor overlay, Slate widget, lifecycle hazards, and cleanup flow for FVineContainerViewportOverlay and SVineContainerViewportOverlay.
svg.text: Source: PCGEditorProcess module, SelectedActorViewportOverlayBase, FVineContainerViewportOverlay, SVineContainerViewportOverlay, AVineContainer
Core rule: old overlay is removed immediately on context change; new overlay creation is deferred through FTSTicker to avoid mutating Slate overlay slots during selection input.

g.text: Skip commandlet, exit, no render, no GEditor
If target changed and overlay exists:
No-op when same target and not forced
Always starts by removing stale overlay

svg.text: Creation is deferred; stale removal can happen immediately on context change.

g.text: Panel must survive selection churn safely
Used when viewport cannot be touched
If target or target already invalid:
Uses cached level/world after actor invalidates
All AddOverlayWidget and RemoveOverlayWidget paths pass through this guard.
Selection can change while Slate is
still processing a click from overlay.
Old overlay may be removed first
Actor can be deleted or invalidated
Exit, non-game thread, or Slate not
Remove overlay and cancel pending refresh
1. Selection event only chooses a candidate AVineContainer and records a queued target.
2. If the candidate differs from the current target, stale overlay is removed before the next add.
3. Next ticker frame calls ApplySelectionRefresh, after Slate input has unwound.
4. ShowOverlayForActor validates actor, LevelEditor, active viewport, and Slate touch safety.
5. Once attached, target actor level/world are cached for later unload/teardown checks.
6. Any map, current-level, actor-delete, world-teardown, or shutdown path clears the cached state.
The overlay is treated as editor UI state, not as part of the AVineContainer actor lifetime.
Stored so RemoveOverlay can detach later
Vine overlay sits above base default

svg.text: The module owns one FVineContainerViewportOverlay. The base class owns editor-lifecycle bookkeeping: selection, queued refresh, viewport attachment, and teardown cleanup.
The Vine subclass only identifies AVineContainer and creates a weak Slate panel. Actor, level, world, viewport, and module lifetimes are intentionally kept separate.
